title: dysoxylum inopinatum
intro: Dysoxylum inopinatum, also known as the Indian rose chestnut, is a plant species that belongs to the family Meliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various parts of the world for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Dysoxylum inopinatum has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is typically prepared in herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used to treat various health issues.
